label: law-sharp
headline_statement: Refuted: in the three-dose binary bounded law with \(d_0=0,d_1=1,d_2=2\), target \(h=1\), stencil \(q_{\cdot 1}=(0,-1,1)\), \(\underline y=0\), \(\overline y=1\), and observed cells \(D=0:(Y_0,Y_1)=(0,0)\), \(D=1:(Y_0,Y_1)=(1,1)\), \(D=2:(Y_0,Y_1)=(0,1)\), the algebraic LP has \(U_1(1;P)=1\) attained by \(\alpha^\dagger_{21}=1\), but every finite bounded latent law preserving \(P\) and Assumptions~\ref{ass:consistency-noanticip}--\ref{ass:regularity} has \(\alpha_{21}=0\) and hence \(\operatorname{ACRT}(d_1;Q)=0\); the endpoint \(1\) is an outer-bound artifact.
witness_construction_if_refuted: Take D in {0,1,2} with equal cell probabilities and deterministic observed cells D=0:(Y0,Y1)=(0,0), D=1:(Y0,Y1)=(1,1), D=2:(Y0,Y1)=(0,1). Then tau=(0,0,1). For target h=1 and q=(0,-1,1), the algebraic array with rows (0,0,0), (0,0,0), (1,1,1) is feasible for L=1 and gives theta=1, so U_1(1;P)=1. But any bounded latent law preserving P has, in the D=1 cell, Y1(d1)=1 and alpha_11=0, hence Y1(d0)=1 almost surely; since Y1(d2)<=1, alpha_21<=0, while Lipschitz regularity and alpha_22=1 imply alpha_21>=0. Thus alpha_21=0 for every law-level completion, and the algebraic endpoint 1 is not law-attainable.
